items.bio: Maya Patel built her voice in packed basement clubs before touring nationally with a set that blends family stories, immigrant humor, and sharp social observations. Her latest hour focuses on modern city life, culture clashes, and finding absurdity in everyday routines.
items.name: Maya Patel
items.blurb: Sharp, autobiographical storytelling with punchy tags.

items.bio: DeAndre Wells is known for high-voltage crowd work and lightning-fast improvisation that turns each show into a one-night-only experience. He has toured with major theater acts and is currently developing a long-form special centered on audience storytelling.
items.name: DeAndre Wells
items.blurb: Big-room energy and high-speed crowd interaction.

items.bio: Tori Banks combines stand-up with rhythmic bits and musical transitions, creating a set that is both theatrical and intimate. Her writing balances dark themes with playful delivery, earning her recurring festival invitations and sold-out late shows.
items.name: Tori Banks
items.blurb: Darkly playful observations with a musical edge.

items.bio: Nico Barr came up through open mic marathons and developed a punchline-heavy style built for fast rooms and late crowds. Offstage, Nico mentors new comics and produces monthly showcases that spotlight emerging local voices.
items.name: Nico Barr
items.blurb: Fast one-liners and Midwest chaos energy.
